title: Check out the first images of Quest shipwreck
gist: A Royal Canadian Geographic Society and Woods Hole expedition released the first images of the Quest, the ship Shackleton died aboard in 1922 and which sank in 1962, revealing worse-than-expected damage from fishing gear alongside a thriving reef ecosystem now living on the wreck.

insights.claim: The Royal Canadian Geographic Society released the first images of the Quest shipwreck -- Ernest Shackleton's last ship, which sank in 1962 -- more than 60 years after it sank and following its initial 2024 sonar discovery.
insights.speaker: Jennifer Ouellette
insights.so_what: Marks the completion of a multi-year, multi-mission search-and-imaging effort into a historically significant wreck.

insights.claim: Ernest Shackleton died aboard the Quest of coronary thrombosis on January 5, 1922, off South Georgia, after falling ill in Rio de Janeiro and making his final diary entry the night before his death.
insights.speaker: Jennifer Ouellette
insights.so_what: Establishes the human/historical stakes that make the wreck's rediscovery notable beyond its physical condition.
insights.quote: Chiefly alcohol, boss, I don’t think it agrees with you,
insights.locator: paragraph beginning 'By 2 am, he was complaining of back pain'

insights.claim: The Royal Canadian Geographic Society invested roughly $365,000 in the search effort, ultimately locating the Quest wreck after 17 hours of sonar scanning following extensive research into ship logs and navigation records.
insights.speaker: Jennifer Ouellette
insights.so_what: Quantifies the scale of investment required for this kind of historical maritime archaeology expedition.

insights.claim: The 2026 follow-up mission, partnered with the Woods Hole Oceanographic Institution, used a Falcon remote-operated vehicle and the ALVIN deep submergence vehicle to explore the wreck, and plans to build a full 3D digital twin of the site using underwater photogrammetry.
insights.speaker: Jennifer Ouellette
insights.so_what: Signals a technically ambitious documentation effort beyond simple imaging, likely yielding a lasting digital archaeological record.
insights.locator: paragraph beginning 'This latest mission, with the Woods Hole Oceanographic Institute'

insights.claim: New imagery shows the Quest is in worse condition than 2024 sonar suggested: its bridge superstructure is missing entirely, and fishing nets, floats, and other bottom-trawling gear are snagged on the stern and much of the starboard side.
insights.speaker: Jennifer Ouellette
insights.so_what: Indicates ongoing physical damage to the wreck from commercial fishing activity in the area, a factor for future preservation efforts.
insights.locator: paragraph beginning 'Initial sonar images in 2024 gave the team hope'

insights.claim: Woods Hole biologist Kirstin Meyer-Kaiser says the Quest wreck has become a thriving underwater ecosystem hosting soft corals and threatened species such as the spotted wolffish, framing the shipwreck as an unintentional artificial reef and potential stepping stone for species spread at a regional scale.
insights.speaker: Jennifer Ouellette
insights.so_what: Highlights an ecological silver lining -- human maritime history creating unintended biodiversity habitat.
insights.quote: It’s really cool to me that the impact of human history is that we’re creating a habitat,
